h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically identify headers and values for each column as prompted.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without any major pedagogical shifts or reversals.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without any major pedagogical shifts or reversals.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without any major pedagogical shifts or reversals.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, so x-axis: “Time (weeks)” means variable = time, unit = weeks?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the tension between the story's context of money and the axis's lack of a printed unit.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The label-only checklist has stopped settling this because the story is supplying money where the axis is silent. Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the standard checklist and introduces a new 'reviewer-proof gate' heuristic to resolve the learner's conflict between the story context and the literal axis label.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the dialogue ends with the learner's turn.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the dialogue ends with the learner's turn.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The label-only checklist has stopped settling this because the story is supplying money where the axis is silent. Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the standard checklist and introduces a new 'reviewer-proof gate' heuristic to resolve the learner's conflict between the story context and the literal axis label.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The label-only checklist has stopped settling this because the story is supplying money where the axis is silent. Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the standard checklist and introduces a new 'reviewer-proof gate' heuristic to resolve the learner's conflict between the story context and the literal axis label.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the visual distraction of the animal pictures while trying to apply the arrow rule.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [slides a blank card over the animal picture, leaving only the two labels and the pencil tail visible]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor dynamically adapts by physically covering the distracting illustration to help the learner focus purely on the text labels.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [slides a blank card over the animal picture, leaving only the two labels and the pencil tail visible]
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor dynamically adapts by physically covering the distracting illustration to help the learner focus purely on the text labels.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [slides a blank card over the animal picture, leaving only the two labels and the pencil tail visible]
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor dynamically adapts by physically covering the distracting illustration to help the learner focus purely on the text labels.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically place the remaining force cards and arrows on the diagram as instructed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent step-by-step instruction strategy as the learner makes no errors and shows no resistance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no learner pivot turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no learner pivot turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent step-by-step instruction strategy as the learner makes no errors and shows no resistance.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent step-by-step instruction strategy as the learner makes no errors and shows no resistance.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with confusing contour lines with physical route paths.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's lingering confusion about route shapes by physically shifting the overlay to decouple the route arrow from the contour line.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's lingering confusion about route shapes by physically shifting the overlay to decouple the route arrow from the contour line.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's lingering confusion about route shapes by physically shifting the overlay to decouple the route arrow from the contour line.
